description: Pre-generated commentary fragments for sentence composition at runtime. Each topic contains openers, bodies, and closers that assemble into complete sentences. Incorporates vocabulary from broadcast commentary, team radio transcripts, coaching channels, and Crew Chief databases. Generated in Kevin's voice: first person, present tense, technically grounded, direct, no filler.

bodies: The car's telling you exactly what happens when you push past the edge — feel it in real time.
The difference between catching it and not is all in how much the wheel's actually talking to you.
That's not a controlled slide — that's the car done with the corner.
Feel the yaw rate hitting the limit and what it takes to pull it back.
The physics of that save are worth explaining — it's left foot, hands, instinct all at once.
You can feel every bit of it through the wheel trying to communicate what just happened.
The car pushed past the limit and demanded immediate correction.
That's genuine oversteer and the recovery is the whole point of practicing this.
closers: That's the car at the limit.

Building temperature in the rubber and clearing the brake dust for what's ahead.

This is where endurance racing separates pace from strategy.

This is where the real driving skill separates people — smooth inputs, read the surface, find the grip.

Smooth is faster in the wet and that's the only thing that doesn't change.

The tyres need building and rushing them is how you lose it early.
Everything feels a step down from normal — pace builds gradually if you're patient.
The window for grip is narrow and the car feels different through every corner.

Cold track means cold tyres and that means reduced grip until things warm up.
Rushing it now will cost you more than being patient in these conditions.

Building heat gradually is the only way through cold conditions.

iRacing saw that and the Safety Rating is going to reflect it — deserved.
In iRacing that's permanent — every contact is recorded and it matters for your rating.

iRacing flagged that and the incident is part of your official record now.
The system saw contact and logged it regardless of circumstances.
That incident just cost you SR and the rating impact is immediate.
